page: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton3 processor family with Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is allocated 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth tests place the instance in the top 25% tier, recording 249.33 GB/sec and 105.99 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance is also notable, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU physics tests scoring in the top 25% tier. However, single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores remain in the average tier.

The instance exhibits clear tradeoffs, particularly in large-scale machine learning workloads. Prompt processing for a large 70B LLM model falls into the bottom 10% tier at 5.18 tokens per second, though smaller model inference remains average. The absence of local storage requires external network-attached storage. Given its high memory bandwidth and strong multi-core capabilities, this instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications that benefit from dedicated ARM cores.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the custom ARM64 architecture and powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processors running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show exceptional memory capabilities, with a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong large-block memory bandwidth. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong Geekbench multi-core and Passmark physics results. However, single-core performance is average, and large LLM inference speeds are poor. This hardware profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded compute workloads, database operations, and static web serving, while being less suitable for large-scale AI model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for highly parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 cores running at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory featuring top-tier memory bandwidth.
Delivers strong multi-core performance and database operation throughput.
Lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage options.
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for multi-threaded compute, static web serving, and database workloads.
